title: Dead Sea Scrolls
wikitext:
{{cleanup}}
[[Image:Tank image 13a dsscroll.jpg|thumb|Image of a page from the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls which flashes during the opening credits]]

The '''Dead Sea Scrolls''' is the in-universe name for a set of documents that serve as the primary guide and philosophy of [[Seele]]. Little is known about them, except for what Seele alludes to during their meetings and what is stated in the [[Classified Information#Dead Sea Scrolls|Classified Information]] section of the [[Neon Genesis Evangelion 2]] video game. 

==In Evangelion==
It is known that the Scrolls are of [[First Ancestral Race|extraterrestrial origin]] and that they serve as a manual on the use and purposes of the contents of [[Black Moon]]s and [[White Moon]]s: a [[Seed of Life]] and the [[Spear of Longinus]].<ref>[[Classified Information#Dead Sea Scrolls|Classified Information]]</ref>. They detail rules on how to use these items, and contain what are often referred to as prophecies on the order and times of release of the [[Angels (Adam's Children)|Angels]].

Seele possessed vast wealth and power, and being founded as a religious organisation, they funded investigations into relics of their faith. This culminated with the excavation of the Dead Sea Scrolls. The irrelevant discoveries were released to the public as the Dead Sea Scrolls, while the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls were kept by Seele. They would later be used to put their ultimate plans of a [[Second Impact]] and [[Third Impact]], followed by [[Human Instrumentality Project|Instrumentality]].

==In Reality==
The real Dead Sea Scrolls were discovered in eleven caves at Qumran in 1947 on the northwestern shore of the Dead Sea. The texts are mostly fragmented, with the exception of a few relatively intact scripts. Scripts and script fragments are numbered according to the cave they were found in. Most scrolls are in Hebrew, while the remainder are mostly in Aramaic and Greek. Fragments of every book of the Old Testament except for the book of Esther have been found among the scrolls.<ref>[http://www.centuryone.com/25dssfacts.html Century One Bookstore: 25 Fascinating Facts About the Dead Sea Scrolls]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20040411155832/http://www.uncc.edu/jdtabor/dssfacts.html The Jewish Roman World of Jesus: Basic Facts Regarding the Dead Sea Scrolls]</ref> 

A number of Dead Sea Scrolls, while classified at the time of Evangelion's production, have since been made available to the public. As such, the mystery surrounding their contents has largely disappeared.<ref>[https://www.deadseascrolls.org.il/explore-the-archive The Dead Sea Scrolls - Explore the Archive]</ref><ref>[https://orion.huji.ac.il/resources/bib/beginner.shtml The Orion Center: Beginner's Guide to Scrolls]</ref><ref>[https://orion.huji.ac.il/resources/NewlyPublishedTexts.shtml The Orion Center: Newly Published Texts]</ref>

==References==
{{reflist}}

"When Aoba called B Wing's construction sloppy he hadn't seen anything like THIS!"

This article or section may require a cleanup to meet a standard of quality that isn't plain embarrassing.
Please discuss this issue on the talk page and/or begin editing the page.

The Dead Sea Scrolls is the in-universe name for a set of documents that serve as the primary guide and philosophy of Seele. Little is known about them, except for what Seele alludes to during their meetings and what is stated in the Classified Information section of the Neon Genesis Evangelion 2 video game.

In Evangelion[edit]

It is known that the Scrolls are of extraterrestrial origin and that they serve as a manual on the use and purposes of the contents of Black Moons and White Moons: a Seed of Life and the Spear of Longinus.[1]. They detail rules on how to use these items, and contain what are often referred to as prophecies on the order and times of release of the Angels.

Seele possessed vast wealth and power, and being founded as a religious organisation, they funded investigations into relics of their faith. This culminated with the excavation of the Dead Sea Scrolls. The irrelevant discoveries were released to the public as the Dead Sea Scrolls, while the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls were kept by Seele. They would later be used to put their ultimate plans of a Second Impact and Third Impact, followed by Instrumentality.

In Reality[edit]

The real Dead Sea Scrolls were discovered in eleven caves at Qumran in 1947 on the northwestern shore of the Dead Sea. The texts are mostly fragmented, with the exception of a few relatively intact scripts. Scripts and script fragments are numbered according to the cave they were found in. Most scrolls are in Hebrew, while the remainder are mostly in Aramaic and Greek. Fragments of every book of the Old Testament except for the book of Esther have been found among the scrolls.[2][3]

A number of Dead Sea Scrolls, while classified at the time of Evangelion's production, have since been made available to the public. As such, the mystery surrounding their contents has largely disappeared.[4][5][6]

References[edit]

  1. ↑ Classified Information
  2. ↑ Century One Bookstore: 25 Fascinating Facts About the Dead Sea Scrolls
  3. ↑ The Jewish Roman World of Jesus: Basic Facts Regarding the Dead Sea Scrolls
  4. ↑ The Dead Sea Scrolls - Explore the Archive
  5. ↑ The Orion Center: Beginner's Guide to Scrolls
  6. ↑ The Orion Center: Newly Published Texts
